topic: fraction multiplication word problem, reasoning about operations
latex:
Gavin plans to repaint some classroom bookcases. All of the bookcases are the same size and each requires $\frac{13}{16}$ gallon of paint. If he's going to paint $5$ bookcases, how many gallons of paint does he need? Complete the sentence below.

To solve, we'll need to $\begin{pmatrix} \text{multiply } 13/16 \text{ by } 5 \\ \text{divide } 5 \text{ by } 13/16 \end{pmatrix}$, because we know the SIZE of the groups and the $\begin{pmatrix} \text{NUMBER of groups} \\ \text{TOTAL gallons of paint} \end{pmatrix}$, but we don't know the $\begin{pmatrix} \text{NUMBER of groups} \\ \text{TOTAL gallons of paint} \end{pmatrix}$.
answer_latex: To solve, we'll need to \textbf{multiply 13/16 by 5}, because we know the SIZE of the groups and the \textbf{NUMBER of groups}, but we don't know the \textbf{TOTAL gallons of paint}.
